scenario_results.reason: The lane produced a sportsbook completion record tied to ticket, line-history, event-result, payout, and reconciliation records.
Missing jurisdiction, house-rule, geolocation, and system-of-record context correctly blocked action and triggered a low-confidence exception path.
Mid-process evidence gaps prevented grading or payout progression and produced a sportsbook-specific blocker summary.
Unsupported wager rescission was refused with an explicit evidence gap and proper compliance-oriented escalation.
Completion without redemption or account-ledger proof was rejected and kept out of a false complete state.
Liability and payout mismatches became an evidence-backed reconciliation exception instead of an unsupported correction.
Suspicious cash and wagering patterns correctly triggered AML or surveillance escalation without unsupported disposition claims.
Self-exclusion, age, or responsible-gaming blockers correctly routed to adjacent ownership rather than normal sportsbook servicing.
Realistic sportsbook terminology and artifact shapes produced a sportsbook-specific execution update rather than a generic operations summary.
Requests for non-sportsbook casino work and trading authority were rejected with a boundary-safe handoff.
Conflicting regulation, house-rule, and tenant-policy guidance correctly triggered escalation instead of confident synthesis.
Retail and mobile workflows across jurisdictions stayed separated so tenant adaptation preserved the sportsbook public baseline.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against a 0.92 minimum threshold for sportsbook execution, payout evidence, reconciliation, boundary safety, and escalation discipline.
Functionality coverage is complete across shared, workflow-phase, and industry-profile abilities.
